Balanced search trees.
DS 2018/2019

Red-black trees
(cid:73) Symmetric binary B-tree, Rudolf Bayer, 1972.
(cid:73) The balancing is maintained by using a coloring of the nodes.
(cid:73) The red-black trees are binary search trees that satisfy the following
properties:
1. a node is colored with red or black;
2. the root and the leaf nodes (nil – that belong to the structure) are
colored with black;
3. if a node is red, then his both children are black;
4. the paths from a node to the boundary nodes have the same number of
black nodes.
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019

Red-black trees
Lemma:
Any subtree of a red-black tree has at least 2bh(v ) − 1 internal nodes,
where:
(cid:73) v the root of the subtree,
(cid:73) bh(v ) the number of black nodes found on a path from v down to a
leaf, not including v ;
Proof.
At class.
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019
4 / 31

Red-black trees
Theorem:
A red-black tree with n internal nodes has the height h ≤ 2 log2(n + 1).
Proof.
According to property 3,
n ≥ 2h/2 − 1 ⇒ h/2 ≤ log2(n + 1) ⇒ h ≤ 2 log2(n + 1).
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019
5 / 31

Red-black trees: operations
Corollary:
In a red-black tree with n nodes, the search operation has a time
complexity of O(log n).
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019
6 / 31

The insert operation
(cid:73) Search for the insertion position and insert the new value as in the
case of ordinary binary search trees.
(cid:73) Color the new node with red.
(cid:73) Restore the red-black properties by recoloring nodes and applying
simple rotations.
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019
7 / 31

The insert operation
(cid:73) Property 1: satisﬁed.
(cid:73) Property 2 – satisﬁed (both children of the inserted node are nil). If
the inserted node is the root → recolor it in black.
(cid:73) Property 4 – satisﬁed (the new red node replaces a leaf).
(cid:73) The property 3 may not hold - if the parent of the node is red.
(cid:73) Move above this situation by recoloring the nodes until it can be
repaired by rotation operations and recoloring.
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019
8 / 31

The insert operation: the restauration of property 3
(cid:73) Case 1: the “uncle” of the inserted node is red →
Recolor the “parent” and the “uncle” in black and the
“grandfather” in red.
(cid:73) Case 2: the “uncle” of the inserted node is black and the inserted
node is the right child of a left child →
Apply a simple left rotation between the current node and the
parent node.
(cid:73) Case 3: the “uncle” of the inserted node is black and the inserted
node is the left child of a left child →
Apply a simple right rotation between the “parent” node and the
“grandfather” node + recolor the “parent” node (in black) and the
“grandfather” node (in red).
Obs.: Apply similar operations for the symmetric case.
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019
9 / 31

The insert operation: algorithm
Procedure insert(t, x)
begin
insBinarySearchTree(t, x)
x → color ← red
while (x! = t and x → pred → color == red) do
if (x → pred == x → pred → pred → left) then
y ← x → pred → pred → right
if (y → color == red) then
Case 1
else
if (x == x → pred → right) then
Case 2
Case 3
else
similarly to the branch ”then”, but interchanging left with right
t → color ← black
end
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019

The delete operation
(cid:73) Similarly to the delete operation from the ordinary binary search trees.
(cid:73) Take into account that the “null” nodes belong to the structure.
(cid:73) After deletion it is possible that the property 4 do not hold any more.
(cid:73) Restore the properties of red-black trees by recoloring nodes and
applying simple rotations.
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019
25 / 31

The delete operation: the restoration of property 4
(cid:73) Case 1: Transform it into one of the cases 2), 3), 4) by a rotation.
(cid:73) Case 2: The node for which the property is not satisﬁed is moved to
the root with one level by recoloring a node.
(cid:73) Case 3: Transform it into case 4) by an interchanging of colors and a
rotation.
(cid:73) Case 4: In this case restore the red-black tree property for the whole
tree.
FII, UAIC
Lecture 10
DS 2018/2019
26 / 31

(cid:73) Applications:
(cid:73) System symbol tables.
(cid:73) Kernel Linux (Completely Fair Scheduler).
(cid:73) Runway reservation system
(cid:73) Java: TreeMap, TreeSet; C++ STL: map, multimap, multiset
Red-black trees
(cid:73) The complexity of insert / delete algorithms: O(log n).
Corollary:
The class of red-black trees is O(log n)–stable.
